channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: UN-backed experts accuse Israel of sexual and gender-based violence in Gaza
item.description: United Nations-backed experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war against Hamas in the Gaza Strip. Israel’s mission to the U.N.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump set to meet with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes just days after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: South Africa-EU summit centers on boosting trade and diplomatic ties as both feel Trump's impact
item.description: Senior European Union officials are in South Africa for a summit with President Cyril Ramaphosa that centers on bolstering trade and diplomatic ties as both feel the impact of the Trump administration’s confrontational foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia says it has retaken Kursk's biggest town from Ukraine as US awaits Putin's ceasefire response
item.description: Russian forces have driven the Ukrainian army out of the biggest town in Russia's Kursk border region, officials claimed Thursday, as U.S. officials sought the Kremlin's response to a proposed 30-day ceasefire in the three-year war that Ukraine has endorsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has retaken Kursk's biggest town from Ukraine as US awaits Putin's ceasefire response
item.description: Russia's Defense Ministry says its forces have driven the Ukrainian army out of the biggest town in Russia’s Kursk border region, as U.S. officials sought the Kremlin’s response to a proposed 30-day ceasefire in the three-year that Ukraine has endorsed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethiopia's Tigray regional government appeals for help after factional fighters seize key towns
item.description: The interim government of Ethiopia’s Tigray region has appealed for the Ethiopian federal government to intervene after a faction of the Tigray People’s Liberation Front seized control of two major towns, leaving several people wounded and raising fears of a return to civil war.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Getachew, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistan's leader to meet with survivors and commandos who ended an insurgents' train attack
item.description: Pakistan’s prime minister traveled to restive southwestern Balochistan province to meet survivors of a train attack and the commandos who rescued over 300 passengers from the insurgents who killed 21 civilians and four troops.
item.dc:creator: Abdul Sattar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio could face an unfriendly reception from close G7 allies over Trump's policies
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio may be walking into unusually unfriendly territory this week when he meets his counterparts from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICC takes custody of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte in crimes against humanity case
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court took custody of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte on Wednesday on a warrant accusing him of crimes against humanity for deadly anti-drugs crackdowns he oversaw while in office.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN launches reform initiative as it nears its 80th anniversary and faces funding cuts
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. chief launched a new initiative Wednesday to reform the United Nations as it approaches its 80th anniversary, saying the 193-member global organization needs an urgent update to deal with major funding reductions and still tackle the world's challenges.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says Canada takeover won't be on G7 agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that talk about the U.S. acquiring Canada will not be a topic of discussion when the Group of Seven (G7) nations meet in Quebec.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Reagan 'would be heartbroken' over Trump isolating US: Patti Davis
item.description: Patti Davis, former President Reagan's daughter, believes that her father would be heartbroken by America's shift towards isolationism under President Trump, as it goes against the values of the country he instilled in her.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Greenland opposition party wins election held in Trump's shadow
item.description: Greenland voters have expressed strong support for sovereignty, with the Demokraatit party winning nearly 30% of the vote, while President Trump continues to push for an annexation of the island with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia waiting for US-Ukraine cease-fire proposal details, Kremlin says
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters Wednesday that Russia must get the details on U.S.-Ukrainian cease-fire talks before commenting on the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's election winners push back against Trump's wish to take control of the island
item.description: A party that favors a gradual path to Greenland’s independence from Denmark has won a surprise victory in parliamentary elections.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis follows Vatican spiritual retreat as doctors say he's no longer in imminent danger
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis participated remotely in the Vatican's spiritual retreat Tuesday after getting good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the respiratory infection that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a mon
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clowns and musicians bring the joy of Carnival to sick children in a Rio hospital
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Clowns complete with red noses and tutus delivered the joy of Carnival to sick children in a Rio de Janeiro hospital on Tuesday, bringing the ebullient festivities normally found on the streets straight to the young patients and their caregivers.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine, US say country open to 30-day ceasefire in war with Russia
item.description: Ukraine says in a joint statement with the United States that it is open to a 30-day ceasefire in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US lifts military freeze on Ukraine in push for 30-day ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. will immediately restore military aid and intelligence sharing to Ukraine following bilateral talks in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, on Tuesday, with Ukraine expressing "readiness" to accept a 30-day ceasefire proposal. "Ukraine expressed readiness to accept the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Evangelical leader says US must protect Syrian Christians from attacks by jihadi terrorists
item.description: A senior evangelical leader has called on the U.S. to not recognize new Syrian regime following bloodshed of religious minorities, including Christians and Alawites, carried out by radical Islamists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte arrested at airport on ICC warrant for crime against humanity
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was arrested at Manila Ninoy Aquino International Airport following an order from the International Criminal Court (ICC).
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says mineral deal ‘not main topic on agenda’ in Ukraine meeting
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio and a U.S. delegation will meet with Ukrainian leaders on Tuesday in Saudi Arabia to discuss ways to end the war between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Philippine President Duterte shrugs off possible arrest by ICC for drug war during trip to Hong Kong
item.description: Ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte visited Hong Kong on Sunday, leading to speculation that he may be attempting to evade a possible arrest warrant by the ICC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania blocks frontrunner from postponed presidential race
item.description: Protests have erupted in Romania after authorities barred Calin Georgescu, a candidate critical of NATO, from running in May's presidential election re-run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization after giving world a sign of his frailty
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis hit the three-week mark Friday in his hospitalization for double pneumonia, in stable condition, receiving therapy but not necessarily improving, after giving the world a tangible indication of just how frail and sick he is.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and MARIA CHENG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Boy with loaded shotgun boarded plane in Australia, tackled by passenger
item.description: A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
